title: Home solar in rural America: a storm just took out your power, now what?
summary: There’s no shortage of advice on how to size a home solar and battery system, but most of it assumes a suburban home with city services and outages measured in hours, not days. Rural homes play by different rules, especially during summer storms. When heavy winds take down power lines and your utility prioritizes restoring urban neighborhoods first, having a home battery isn’t about convenience — it’s about damage control.

title: The Genesis GV90 looks huge charging next to the Kia EV9
summary: The GV90 is set to arrive as the biggest, most prestige Genesis electric SUV to date. With its official debut coming up, the flagship EV was spotted charging alongside a Kia EV9, revealing its true size.

title: Waymo taps fleet giant Element to scale its robotaxi service
summary: Waymo has tapped fleet management company Element to help keep its robotaxis charged, maintained, and on the road as the company expands into more cities.

title: BYD’s new magnetic EV device lets you control your car on the go
summary: BYD is about to introduce a new detachable magnetic smart device that lets you control your vehicle remotely at the push of a button.

title: Rivian lays off hundreds, under 2% of staff, a week after R2 launch
summary:
Rivian laid off hundreds of workers on Tuesday, or less than 2% of its workforce, just one week after it began deliveries of its all-important R2 SUV.

The electric automaker confirmed the cuts hit its service and customer teams, which include sales and marketing, as it tries to claw its way to a profit it has yet to ever post.

title: Bluetti 2,016Wh+ FridgePower station exclusive launch deals from $807, Segway Xyber e-bike + extra battery $1,400 off at low, more
summary:
We’ve got an exciting round of Green Deals for you today, starting with the exclusive launch savings on Bluetti’s 2,016Wh to 8,064Wh FridgePower Portable Power Station and bundles starting from $807. Right beside it is the Segway Xyber Electric Bike getting a FREE extra battery ($1,000 value) at its $2,900 low, as well as a new low price hitting the Xafari All-Terrain Cruiser e-bike for $1,550. We also have Jackery’s updated Early Prime Father’s Day merged sale lineup with bonus extra savings starting from $199, as well as the best price in 12 months on EGO’s 22-inch Cordless Electric Select Cut Touch Drive Lawn Mower with a 10.0Ah battery, and more waiting for you below. Don’t forget about all the hangover deals either, which have been collected together at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s Early Prime Day flash sales from Jackery and Anker SOLIX (ending tonight), the $400 markdown on Navee’s UT5 Ultra X Electric SuperScooter, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Hyundai is closing in on Chevy as the #2 EV brand in the US
summary: Hyundai is on track for its sixth straight year of record sales in the US. In the EV market, Hyundai has its sights set on Chevrolet as it looks to become the second-best-selling brand behind Tesla.

title: Toyota’s new EVs are catching on as registrations jump 225% in April
summary: With three fully electric SUVs now on sale, Toyota’s EV registrations surged 225% in April.

title: Kia’s electric van delivers more driving range in the real world at 260 miles
summary: It’s no wonder the PV5 is flying off dealer lots. With more than enough driving range, fast charging, and a high-tech, spacious interior, Kia’s electric van may be the ultimate people mover.

title: Tesla ‘Robotaxis’ are not crashing because they are not running
summary:
Tesla didn’t report a single at-fault “Robotaxi” crash in the latest NHTSA autonomous-vehicle data — its only fresh incident was a Model Y getting rear-ended while stopped, clearly the other driver’s fault.

That sounds like good news for Tesla’s safety record. But live fleet data points to the real reason the crashes have dried up: Tesla’s robotaxis are barely running, and the active fleet is shrinking a year into the program.

title: Rivian Lays Off Hundreds Of Workers A Week After R2 Launch
summary: Rivian cut employees from its service and customer division on Tuesday, the The Wall Street Journal reported.

title: The Audi A6 Allroad Is Now A Plug-In Hybrid Antidote To Boring SUVs
summary: Thanks to a big battery pack, the new A6 Allroad wagon can deliver nearly 60 miles of electric range on the WLTP cycle.

title: Telo's Tiny Electric Truck Moves Closer To Reality With An Important New Partner
summary: Telo's adorable truck just hit a major milestone as the company finds a manufacturing partner for the EV's body structure.

title: Full Tesla Cybercab Specs: EPA Documents Confirm Battery Size, Weight, Power, And Preliminary Range
summary: The first front-wheel-drive Tesla will weigh way less than a Model 3 but still offer plenty of range.

title: A Chinese Car Brand Says It Hired A Top Ferrari Designer. Ferrari Wants A Name
summary: Luxeed says it hired a former Ferrari chief designer. Ferrari’s China PR boss publicly challenged the claim by asking who that was.

title: Ford's $30,000 Electric Pickup: Here’s A Closer Look
summary: We get a glimpse at the design sketches as the first prototypes hit the public roads for testing.

title: This Low-Mileage 2025 Tesla Model Y Lost A Lot Of Range Fast
summary: After 18 months and just over 13,000 miles, Tesla’s own battery-health test showed a result its owner called unacceptable.

summary: It looks like a close-to-production preview of the production BMW i3 M, but the specs are still under wraps.

title: The Rivian R2 Is Now Shipping With A Rare Accessory The Competition Forgot Existed
summary: The R2 is billed as a relatively affordable adventure-ready EV. For an extra $755, owners can get some peace of mind.
